title: Mock Test 09 — Full Syllabus High Difficulty

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Notice: 'During a fire, use the stairs; do not use the lift.' The assumption underlying this notice is that
options: lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire
there is never a real fire
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An assumption is what must be taken for granted for the advice to make sense.

**Working.** Advising against lifts during a fire presumes lifts are unsafe then.

**Answer.** lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'The introduction of automated haulage trucks reduced accidents in the pit by 40%. However, it also displaced a number of human drivers, prompting the company to launch a retraining programme.'

It can be inferred that the company:
options: sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce
abandoned automation because of the job losses
increased the number of human drivers it employed
ignored the workers displaced by automation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An inference must follow logically from stated facts.

**Working.** Launching a retraining programme in response to displaced drivers shows the company addressed automation's workforce impact.

**Answer.** sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A clock loses $5$ minutes every hour. After $12$ hours of running, it is _____ minutes behind the correct time.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total loss $=$ rate of loss $\times$ time.

**Working.** $5\,\text{min/h}\times 12\,\text{h}=60$ minutes.

**Answer.** $60$ minutes.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Five people are ranked by height: P is taller than Q, Q is taller than R, S is shorter than R, and T is the tallest. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Chain the inequalities into one order.

**Working.** $T>P>Q>R>S$, so S is the shortest.

**Answer.** S.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The total surface area of a cube of side $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Surface area of a cube $=6\,(\text{side})^2$.

**Working.** $6\times 5^2 = 6\times 25 = 150$.

**Answer.** $150$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the synonym of 'METICULOUS'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Meticulous' describes someone who shows great attention to detail.

**Working.** 'Careful' captures this meaning; the remaining options describe the opposite trait.

**Answer.** careful.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Triangle is to $3$ sides as the pentagon shown is to

figure.caption: A regular pentagon.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='60,15 110,52 91,108 29,108 10,52' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The analogy maps a polygon to its number of sides.

**Working.** A pentagon has $5$ sides.

**Answer.** 5 sides.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: X is the brother of the son of Y's son. How is X related to Y?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve generations from the inside out.

**Working.** 'The son of Y's son' is Y's grandson; X is his brother, hence also Y's grandson.

**Answer.** Grandson.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of ways to choose $2$ items from $6$ distinct items is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order does not matter, so use $\binom{n}{r}$.

**Working.** $\binom{6}{2}=\dfrac{6\times5}{2}=15$.

**Answer.** $15$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The arrow shown points to the right (east). After rotating it $90^\circ$ anticlockwise, it points

figure.caption: A horizontal arrow pointing to the right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='120,40 105,32 105,48' fill='#ef4444'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $90^\circ$ anticlockwise turn sends east to north.

**Working.** Right rotated anticlockwise becomes upward.

**Answer.** Upward (north).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: To pass $60\,\text{m}^3$/s at a limiting velocity of $5\,\text{m/s}$, the airway cross-section must be at least _____ m$^2$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A=\dfrac{Q}{v}$.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{60}{5}=12.$$

**Answer.** $12\,\text{m}^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Gale's traverse table is used to
options: compute the calorific value of coal
systematically compute latitudes, departures, corrections and independent coordinates of traverse stations
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gale's table organises traverse computation.

**Reasoning.** It tabulates each line's length, bearing, latitude, departure, corrections (Bowditch/transit) and the running (independent) coordinates, providing a clear computation and closure check.

**Answer.** Tabular traverse computation.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span $L=4\ \mathrm{m}$ carries a uniformly distributed load $w=10\ \mathrm{kN/m}$ (see figure). The maximum bending moment, in kN·m, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='210' y2='80' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='4'/><path d='M30 80 L22 95 L38 95 Z' fill='#475569'/><path d='M210 80 L202 95 L218 95 Z' fill='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='40' y1='45' x2='40' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/><line x1='73' y1='45' x2='73' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/><line x1='106' y1='45' x2='106' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/><line x1='139' y1='45' x2='139' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/><line x1='172' y1='45' x2='172' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/><line x1='200' y1='45' x2='200' y2='74' marker-end='url(#ud)'/></g><defs><marker id='ud' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='78' y='38' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>w = 10 kN/m</text><text x='100' y='112' font-size='9'>L = 4 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam, UDL

questions.solution:
**Concept.** UDL on a simple span: $M_{max}=\dfrac{wL^2}{8}$.

**Working.**
$$M_{max}=\frac{10\times4^2}{8}=\frac{160}{8}=20\ \text{kN·m}$$

**Answer.** $20$ kN·m.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplacian $\nabla^{2}f$ of $f=x^{2}+y^{2}+z^{2}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla^{2}f=f_{xx}+f_{yy}+f_{zz}$.

**Working.**
$$f_{xx}=f_{yy}=f_{zz}=2\;\Rightarrow\;\nabla^{2}f=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A dry cohesionless infinite slope has $\phi=38^\circ$ and slope angle $\beta=25^\circ$. The factor of safety $\tan\phi/\tan\beta$ is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\beta}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{\tan 38^\circ}{\tan 25^\circ}=\frac{0.781}{0.466}=1.68.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.68$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Blackdamp (chokedamp) is
options: an irrespirable mixture mainly of carbon dioxide and nitrogen that extinguishes a flame
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Blackdamp is oxygen-deficient air.

**Reasoning.** It is air depleted of oxygen and enriched in CO₂ and N₂ (e.g. from oxidation of coal/timber); it does not support flame or life, hence 'choke' damp.

**Answer.** CO₂ + N₂ irrespirable mixture.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A pit will produce $4$ million tonnes of ore and $12$ million tonnes of waste. The overall stripping ratio is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{SR}=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{12}{4}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The risk of spontaneous heating (fire) in a longwall goaf is greatest when
options: the coal has low proneness and the goaf is air-tight
the coal is highly prone to oxidation and air leaks slowly through the goaf
the panel is fully stowed with sand
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Spontaneous heating needs reactive coal plus a slow air supply.

**Reasoning.** Crushed, oxidation-prone coal in the goaf generates heat; if air leaks through at a rate fast enough to supply oxygen but too slow to carry heat away, temperature builds to ignition. Sealing or flooding the goaf reduces the risk.

**Answer.** Prone coal with slow air leakage through the goaf.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A gravimetric respirable-dust sampler determines dust concentration by
options: drawing a known air volume through a size-selector (cyclone) and weighing the dust collected on a filter
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Concentration $=$ mass collected ÷ air volume.

**Reasoning.** A pre-selector (cyclone) passes only the respirable fraction to a weighed filter; the mass gain divided by the metered air volume gives mg/m³ — the standard exposure measure.

**Answer.** Weigh filtered respirable dust per air volume sampled.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following are measures of CENTRAL TENDENCY?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central tendency vs dispersion.

**Working.**
- Mean, median, mode locate the centre — TRUE.
- Standard deviation measures spread (dispersion) — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Using the net-present-value criterion, an independent project is acceptable when its NPV is
options: greater than or equal to zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Positive NPV means returns exceed the required rate.

**Working.** Accept if $\text{NPV}\ge 0$.

**Answer.** greater than or equal to zero.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The most abundant group of rock-forming minerals in the earth's crust is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Silicon and oxygen dominate the crust.

**Reasoning.** Silicates, built on the SiO₄ tetrahedron, make up the bulk of crustal rocks; feldspars alone form ~50%, followed by quartz, pyroxenes, amphiboles and micas.

**Answer.** Silicates.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In plaster (mudcap) secondary blasting, the charge is
options: placed inside a drilled hole in the boulder
laid on the boulder surface and covered with stiff clay/mud — no drilling required
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mudcapping needs no drilling.

**Reasoning.** A charge is placed on the boulder and confined by a mud cap; the detonation breaks the boulder by surface shock. It saves drilling but uses more explosive than blockholing.

**Answer.** Charge on the surface under a mud cover.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The present worth of a perpetual annual income of $\textsf{Rs}\,8{,}000$ at $8\%$ per year is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perpetuity $P=\dfrac{A}{i}$.

**Working.**
$$P=\frac{8000}{0.08}=100000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Foliation in metamorphic rocks such as slate, schist and gneiss is
options: the bedding inherited unchanged from sediments
a planar arrangement of mineral grains developed under directed (differential) pressure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Directed stress aligns minerals.

**Reasoning.** Platy/elongate minerals grow or rotate perpendicular to the maximum stress, giving slaty cleavage (slate), schistosity (schist) or gneissic banding (gneiss).

**Answer.** Pressure-aligned planar fabric.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the Mean Value Theorem, the value $c\in(1,3)$ satisfying $f'(c)=\dfrac{f(3)-f(1)}{3-1}$ for $f(x)=x^{2}$ is

figure.caption: Mean Value Theorem: the tangent at c is parallel to the secant joining the endpoints.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 135 Q130 20 215 70' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='135' x2='215' y2='70' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='95' y1='90' x2='185' y2='57' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='140' cy='73' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='110' y='40' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>secant</text><text x='120' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>tangent at c</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** MVT: there exists $c$ with $f'(c)$ equal to the average rate of change.

**Working.**
$$\frac{f(3)-f(1)}{2}=\frac{9-1}{2}=4,\qquad f'(c)=2c=4\Rightarrow c=2$$

**Answer.** $c=2$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The 'grade' of a coal, as distinct from its rank, refers to its
options: quality as judged by the amount of mineral matter / ash (and hence useful heat content)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Grade reflects purity, not maturity.

**Reasoning.** Grade is governed by ash (mineral matter) content; Indian non-coking coals are graded by gross calorific value. Two coals of the same rank can differ in grade.

**Answer.** Quality set by ash/mineral content.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A high wet-bulb temperature is dangerous in hot mines because it
options: indicates high humidity, which suppresses evaporation of sweat and so impairs the body's cooling
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sweat cooling depends on humidity.

**Reasoning.** The body sheds heat largely by evaporating sweat; when the air is humid (high wet-bulb) evaporation slows, the worker cannot cool down, and heat stress/heat stroke becomes likely.

**Answer.** High humidity suppresses evaporative cooling.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The area of an irregular figure bounded by offsets taken at regular intervals along a base line is commonly found by
options: the trapezoidal rule or Simpson's rule
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Numerical integration of offsets gives area.

**Reasoning.** Treating the boundary between offsets as straight gives the trapezoidal rule; as a parabola gives Simpson's rule (more accurate for curved boundaries, needs even number of intervals).

**Answer.** Trapezoidal / Simpson's rule.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A higher RQD value generally indicates
options: a more massive, less fractured rock mass
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD measures the percentage of sound long core pieces.

**Working.** Higher RQD means fewer fractures and better quality.

**Answer.** a more massive, less fractured rock mass.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dumper's cycle is: loading 2 min, loaded haul 5 min, dumping 1 min, empty return 3 min, spotting 1 min. The total cycle time, in minutes, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cycle time is the sum of all phase times.

**Working.**
$$t_c=2+5+1+3+1=12\ \text{min}$$

**Answer.** 12 min.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\lambda=2$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, then the corresponding eigenvalue of $A^{3}-2A$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $Av=\lambda v$, then $p(A)v=p(\lambda)v$ for any polynomial $p$.

**Working.**
$$p(\lambda)=\lambda^{3}-2\lambda=2^{3}-2(2)=8-4=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: An 'offset' in chain surveying is
options: the error in the chain length
the lateral distance of a detail point from the chain (survey) line
the difference of fore and back bearings
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Offsets locate detail relative to the chain line.

**Reasoning.** Perpendicular (or oblique) offsets give the distance of features such as buildings or boundaries from the main line, so they can be plotted; perpendicular offsets are preferred for accuracy.

**Answer.** Lateral distance of a point from the chain line.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: An unconformity in a rock sequence represents
options: a continuous deposition with no time gap
a buried erosion (or non-deposition) surface marking a significant gap in the geological record
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unconformities mark missing time.

**Reasoning.** Older rocks were uplifted/eroded (or deposition ceased) before younger rocks were laid on top; the surface between records a hiatus.

**Answer.** Buried erosion surface (time gap).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A modern methanometer measures methane concentration by
options: catalytic (pellistor) oxidation of methane on a heated catalyst, sensed as a resistance change
measuring the rotation of an anemometer vane
the colour change of a wet-bulb thermometer
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Catalytic combustion sensing.

**Reasoning.** Methane burns on a heated catalytic bead (pellistor); the heat raises its resistance proportionally to the gas %, giving a direct CH₄ reading. (Infrared sensors are also used.)

**Answer.** Catalytic (pellistor) oxidation.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The specific gravity of rock solids with grain density $2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$ (water density $1000\,\text{kg/m}^3$) is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $G_s=\dfrac{\rho_s}{\rho_w}$.

**Working.**
$$G_s=\frac{2650}{1000}=2.65.$$

**Answer.** $2.65$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the Cauchy–Euler equation $x^{2}y''+xy'-y=0$, the positive value of $m$ in the trial solution $y=x^{m}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Substitute $y=x^{m}$ to obtain the indicial equation.

**Working.**
$$m(m-1)+m-1=m^{2}-1=0\Rightarrow m=\pm1$$
The positive root is $m=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the stress state $\sigma_1=80\ \text{MPa}$, $\sigma_3=20\ \text{MPa}$, compute the normal stress on a plane whose normal makes $\theta=30^\circ$ with $\sigma_1$ (MPa).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_n=\dfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}+\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}\cos2\theta$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_n=50+30\cos60^\circ=50+30(0.5)=65\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $65$ MPa.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using $S_r=\dfrac{wG_s}{e}$ with $w=0.08$, $G_s=2.7$ and $e=0.30$, the degree of saturation is _____ %.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: With $FC=\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ and contribution $\textsf{Rs}\,40$/unit, the output needed for a target profit of $\textsf{Rs}\,2{,}00{,}000$ is _____ units.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Units $=\dfrac{FC+\text{profit}}{\text{contribution}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{500000+200000}{40}=\frac{700000}{40}=17500.$$

**Answer.** $17{,}500$ units.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Using $R=\dfrac{k\,P\,L}{A^3}$ with friction factor $k=0.01\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^4$, perimeter $P=10\,\text{m}$, length $L=500\,\text{m}$ and area $A=6\,\text{m}^2$, the airway resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Atkinson resistance $R=\dfrac{kPL}{A^3}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\frac{0.01\times10\times500}{6^3}=\frac{50}{216}=0.23.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.23\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The preferred hierarchy for controlling occupational noise is
options: ear protection first, then everything else
control at the source, then along the transmission path (enclosure/barrier), and only then protection at the receiver
ignore the source and rely solely on distance
increase ventilation to disperse the noise
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Engineering controls precede personal protection.

**Reasoning.** Reducing noise at the machine (source) is most effective; if residual noise remains, enclosures/barriers attenuate the path; hearing protection at the worker is the last resort.

**Answer.** Source → path → receiver.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: 1,000,000 bank m³ of waste, swelling by 25%, is placed on an external dump. The loose dump volume, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose volume $=$ bank volume $\times(1+\text{swell})$.

**Working.**
$$V_L=1{,}000{,}000\times1.25=1{,}250{,}000\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 1,250,000 m³.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: For the M/M/1 loader system ($\lambda=6$/h, $\mu=10$/h) shown, compute the average number of trucks waiting in the queue $L_q$.

figure.caption: Single-server queue
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='45' width='18' height='24'/><rect x='62' y='45' width='18' height='24'/><rect x='84' y='45' width='18' height='24'/></g><circle cx='150' cy='57' r='22' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='150' y='61' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>loader</text><line x1='10' y1='57' x2='38' y2='57' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#qa)'/><line x1='174' y1='57' x2='230' y2='57' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#qa)'/><defs><marker id='qa' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='12' y='44' font-size='8'>lam=6</text><text x='190' y='44' font-size='8'>mu=10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For M/M/1, $L_q = \dfrac{\rho^2}{1-\rho}$.

**Working.**
$$\rho = 0.6,\qquad L_q = \frac{0.6^2}{1-0.6} = \frac{0.36}{0.4} = 0.9$$

**Answer.** $L_q = \mathbf{0.9}$ trucks.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Sub-grade (sub-drilling) drilling below the bench floor is provided to
options: ensure the charge breaks the rock down to floor level and avoids a hard toe/high bottom
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The charge is least effective near its bottom.

**Reasoning.** Drilling a little below grade places explosive energy at floor level so the rock shears to the design floor, preventing toe (unbroken bottom) that would hamper loading and the next bench.

**Answer.** Break to floor level / avoid a hard toe.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Each 12 m hole takes 4 min to drill plus 1 min to reposition. In a 6 h (360 min) drilling shift, the number of holes completed is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holes $=\dfrac{\text{available time}}{\text{time per hole}}$.

**Working.**
$$t_{hole}=4+1=5\ \text{min},\quad N=\frac{360}{5}=72$$

**Answer.** 72 holes.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Two events $A$ and $B$ are independent if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Definition of statistical independence.

**Working.** Independence means the joint probability factorises: $P(A\cap B)=P(A)P(B)$.

**Answer.** $P(A\cap B)=P(A)\,P(B)$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A flame safety lamp is traditionally used in a mine to
options: detect methane (by cap height) and oxygen deficiency
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The flame responds to gas in air.

**Working.** A methane cap appears above the flame; a low flame indicates oxygen deficiency.

**Answer.** detect methane and oxygen deficiency.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The residual strength of a rock is
options: the strength sustained after the peak, on continued shearing
the strength at first crack initiation
always greater than the peak strength
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond peak, strength drops to a residual plateau.

**Working.** It is the post-peak strength maintained during continued displacement.

**Answer.** the strength sustained after the peak, on continued shearing.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: To raise the factor of safety of pillars in a deepening mine, one can
options: narrow the pillars and widen the galleries
enlarge the pillars (lower the extraction ratio), reducing pillar stress and raising w/h
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $FS$ rises with strength and falls with stress.

**Reasoning.** Larger pillars lower $e$ (so $\sigma_p=\sigma_v/(1-e)$ falls) and raise $w/h$ (so $S_p$ rises) — both increase $FS$. The penalty is lower recovery.

**Answer.** Enlarge pillars / reduce extraction ratio.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A plane truss has $m=11$ members, $j=7$ joints and $r=3$ reaction components. The value of $m+r-2j$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $m+r-2j$ measures static determinacy of a plane truss.

**Working.**
$$11+3-2(7)=14-14=0.$$

**Answer.** $0$ (statically determinate).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The 'effective temperature' (ET) index is
options: an empirical index combining dry-bulb, wet-bulb and air velocity to represent the thermal sensation felt by a worker
the temperature of the rock at depth
the boiling point of mine water
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ET expresses combined thermal effect as one number.

**Reasoning.** It merges temperature, humidity and air speed into an equivalent still-air, saturated temperature giving the same sensation — used to set comfort/working limits underground.

**Answer.** Empirical index of dry-bulb, wet-bulb and velocity.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Contour mining is practised when a seam
options: lies flat under gently rolling plains
outcrops along the side of a hill, the cut following the contour
is buried more than 300 m below the surface
dips vertically into a deep basin
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In hilly terrain a seam outcrops along the hillside at a fairly constant elevation.

**Reasoning.** Contour mining follows that outcrop around the hill along the contour line, removing overburden from the cropline inward until the stripping ratio becomes uneconomic.

**Answer.** Seam outcropping along a hillside, cut following the contour.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A $2\,\text{kg}$ body is accelerated from rest to $10\,\text{m/s}$. The impulse delivered to it is _____ N·s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Impulse $=$ change in momentum $=m\,\Delta v$.

**Working.**
$$J=2(10-0)=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{N·s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A 'grade contour' (contour gradient) marked on the ground is a line that
options: maintains a constant specified gradient (slope) along its length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A grade line keeps slope constant, not height.

**Reasoning.** Used to lay out haul roads, drains and inclines, the grade contour rises (or falls) at a fixed rate; its plan position is found by stepping a fixed horizontal distance per contour interval.

**Answer.** Constant-gradient line.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers $40\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ against a pressure of $500\ \text{Pa}$ (figure). Compute the air power (kW).

figure.caption: Fan delivering air
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='70' cy='65' r='35' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><g stroke='#475569' fill='#bfdbfe'><path d='M70 65 L70 35 L82 50 Z'/><path d='M70 65 L100 65 L85 77 Z'/><path d='M70 65 L70 95 L58 80 Z'/><path d='M70 65 L40 65 L55 53 Z'/></g><line x1='105' y1='65' x2='180' y2='65' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='4'/><text x='30' y='120' font-size='8'>p=500 Pa, Q=40 m^3/s</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=p\times Q$.

**Working.**
$$AP=500\times40=20000\ \text{W}=20\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $20$ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: When flow divides between two parallel airways of equal resistance, each carries _____ percent of the total.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equal resistances split flow equally.

**Working.** Each branch takes half.

**Answer.** $50\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The figure shows two airways connected in series between junctions A and C, of resistances $R_1=2$ and $R_2=3\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$. The equivalent resistance of the path A–C, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resistances in series add directly: $R_{eq}=R_1+R_2$.

**Working.**
$$R_{eq}=2+3=5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$$

**Answer.** $5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: For the same network (critical path $15$ days, lower path $4+5=9$ days), compute the total float available on the lower path (days).

figure.caption: Total float on a non-critical path
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='100' r='14'/><circle cx='200' cy='70' r='14'/></g><g stroke='#dc2626' marker-end='url(#na)'><line x1='44' y1='75' x2='96' y2='95'/><line x1='124' y1='95' x2='186' y2='75'/></g><g stroke='#475569' marker-end='url(#na)'><line x1='44' y1='65' x2='96' y2='45'/><line x1='124' y1='45' x2='186' y2='65'/></g><defs><marker id='na' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='7' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='150' y='112' font-size='8'>lower path = 9</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total float $=$ critical-path duration $-$ path duration for parallel chains sharing the same end nodes.

**Working.**
$$\text{float}=15-9=6\ \text{days}$$

**Answer.** $6$ days.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Present Worth
questions.stem: The present worth of $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$ to be received $10$ years hence at $12\%$ is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A charge column of 8.2 m has a linear charge concentration of 15 kg/m. The explosive mass per hole, in kg, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Charge mass $=$ column length $\times$ linear concentration.

**Working.**
$$Q=8.2\times15=123\ \text{kg}$$

**Answer.** 123 kg.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using Palmstrom's relation $\text{RQD}=115-3.3\,J_v$ for a volumetric joint count $J_v=10\,\text{joints/m}^3$, the RQD is _____ percent.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bank volume of $100\ \mathrm{m^3}$ of rock has a swell of $25\%$ when excavated (figure). Compute the loose (in-truck) volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).

figure.caption: Bank versus loose volume
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='60' width='60' height='60' fill='#a8a29e' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='130' y='40' width='70' height='80' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='135' font-size='8'>bank 100 m^3</text><text x='130' y='135' font-size='8'>loose +25%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose volume $=$ bank volume $\times(1+\text{swell})$.

**Working.**
$$V_L=100\times1.25=125\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $125\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The 'degree of a curve' is defined as the angle subtended at the centre by a
options: standard arc (or chord) length such as 30 m
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Degree of curve is an alternative to radius.

**Reasoning.** It is the central angle for a standard arc (30 m in metric practice); a sharper curve (smaller R) has a larger degree. $R=\dfrac{1718.9}{D}$ for a 30 m arc.

**Answer.** Central angle for a standard arc/chord.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: As an open-pit mine is deepened, the stripping ratio generally
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deeper benches expose more waste per tonne of ore.

**Working.** The waste-to-ore ratio rises with depth.

**Answer.** increases.
